purpose: Current-phase task checklist generated from the agent-maintained source coverage matrix and platform capability check. Use it before editing and update the matrix/check after work.
instructions: Start with implementationGroups as the current phase implementation contract: implement each group, then use mustComplete entries as exact row-level checks.

Use planContract as upstream planning ownership evidence; resolve its alignmentFindings by updating plannedPhase/matrix/platform evidence or by documenting sourceOverride/analysisGap when source inspection disagrees.
Keep grouped business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints; avoid closing unrelated features only through root/shell/whole-file endpoints.
Merge source files only when it improves target clarity and still preserves per-feature evidence, tests, and controllable file size.
Treat mustComplete entries as the current phase's executable row-level task list.
Use behaviorAudit as the current phase behavior audit; it checks closed critical/high rows for source behavior, target behavior, parity, and verification evidence.

Use uiContract entries as static-analysis planning aids, not complete UI specifications: preserve listed source-visible screen structure, route/back behavior, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence needs, then re-read source/resources/navigation for UI behavior the static analysis may have missed.
Do not close the phase while current-phase critical/high entries are open, weakly deferred, or missing evidence.
For every closed critical/high source feature, record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence in source-coverage-matrix.json.
A build pass alone is not per-feature verification; cite tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence for behavior closure.
Use implemented-simplified only with concrete simplification reason, preserved-behavior explanation, target endpoints or tests, and source/platform evidence.

If a suitable HarmonyOS/Cangjie API, Kit, example, or package exists for a platform capability, use it or record a platform replacement; do not close the item with local mock data, sample data, in-memory simulation, or another fallback.
Use deferred only for concrete blockers; otherwise implement faithfully or platform-replace with evidence.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json/md and platform-capability-check.json/md before phase completion.
If this checklist is empty but the phase has real scope in plan.md/plan.json, assign plannedPhase values first.

platformDeferredOrFallback.sourceEvidenceSummary: Detected 87 source artifact(s) with terms: permissions, permission
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection found no Android runtime permission request or protected-API flow in the startup/session/navigation implementation scope: `presentation/MainActivity.kt`, `presentation/common/SplashViewModel.kt`, `presentation/util/Navigation.kt`, `domain/repository/HomeRepository.kt`, `domain/repository/LoginRepository.kt`, and `domain/repository/UserDataRepository.kt` contain startup routing, repository contracts, and shell behavior only.

Repository-wide source grep over `D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java` found DataStore and `Application` usage only for this phase surface; it did not find `requestPermissions`, `rememberLauncherForActivityResult`, `ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission`, `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `POST_NOTIFICATIONS`, `Service`, `BroadcastReceiver`, `WorkManager`, or notification APIs in the inspected phase sources.

Current Cangjie SDK does provide a callable runtime-permission path through `AtManager.requestPermissionsFromUser(context: UIAbilityContext, permissionList: Array<Permissions>, requestCallback: AsyncCallback<PermissionRequestResult>)` and `PermissionRequestResult`, so this row is closed as phase-specific not-applicable rather than missing-platform-support.
platformDeferredOrFallback.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie AbilityKit permission APIs exist (`ohos.ability_access_ctrl.AtManager`, `ohos.security.permission_request_result.PermissionRequestResult`) but are not required by phase-02 startup/session/navigation source behavior.
platformDeferredOrFallback.targetStrategy: Treat the phase-02 permission requirement as a source-analysis false positive. Do not add permission prompts or protected-access scaffolding during startup/session/navigation implementation unless later source-backed phases introduce real protected APIs.
tests: phase-02 source grep confirms no permission request path in startup/session/navigation sources
platformDeferredOrFallback.minimumParity: Request required permission, handle grant/deny, show user-facing blocked state, and avoid accessing protected APIs before authorization.
platformDeferredOrFallback.notes: Phase-02 source review confirms runtime permissions are not required by the startup/session/navigation batch. The accepted phase-02 test build report is PASS, so this row no longer relies on stale blocked-test notes.

platformResolved.capabilityName: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
platformResolved.sourceEvidenceSummary: Detected 10 source artifact(s) with terms: notification_widget, notification, remoteviews, platform_component, service, receiver, background_work
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection confirmed real app-entry and lifecycle scope, but not Android background services/receivers/widgets: `FoodikeApp.kt` defines `class FoodikeApp : Application()`, `MainActivity.kt` defines a single `ComponentActivity` with `installSplashScreen().setKeepOnScreenCondition { splashViewModel.isLoading.value }`, and `SplashViewModel.kt` resolves onboarding vs home from persisted login state before normal UI rendering.

Current Cangjie SDK declares HarmonyOS app-entry and lifecycle APIs through `UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, and ability startup methods such as `startAbility` / `startAbilityForResult`, which are the direct target replacement for the source `Application` + `Activity` startup surface.

HarmonyOS Cangjie navigation docs recommend ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app page routing, so the source single-activity navigation shell should be implemented as HarmonyOS app bootstrap plus ArkUI navigation rather than Android activity/navigation infrastructure.
platformResolved.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie AbilityKit app-entry APIs (`ohos.app.ability.ui_ability.UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, `Want`) plus ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app startup and route flow
platformResolved.targetStrategy: Resolve the source Android platform component surface as a HarmonyOS platform replacement: model app startup in the target `UIAbility`/descriptor entry, keep splash-like gating inside bootstrap state before rendering the main route, and implement onboarding/home navigation using the existing ArkUI route shell instead of Android `Application`, `ComponentActivity`, and `core-splashscreen` APIs.
platformResolved.minimumParity: Preserve user-visible background behavior, notification/widget state, scheduling semantics, and lifecycle entry points when in source scope.
platformResolved.notes: Phase-02 only needs app entry and startup lifecycle gating. The platform replacement is carried by precise entry/startup endpoints, and the accepted phase-02 test build report now backs the startup-store contract evidence.

platformContracts.capabilityName: Runtime permissions and protected platform access
platformContracts.minimumParity: Request required permission, handle grant/deny, show user-facing blocked state, and avoid accessing protected APIs before authorization.
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection found no Android runtime permission request or protected-API flow in the startup/session/navigation implementation scope: `presentation/MainActivity.kt`, `presentation/common/SplashViewModel.kt`, `presentation/util/Navigation.kt`, `domain/repository/HomeRepository.kt`, `domain/repository/LoginRepository.kt`, and `domain/repository/UserDataRepository.kt` contain startup routing, repository contracts, and shell behavior only.

Repository-wide source grep over `D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java` found DataStore and `Application` usage only for this phase surface; it did not find `requestPermissions`, `rememberLauncherForActivityResult`, `ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission`, `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `POST_NOTIFICATIONS`, `Service`, `BroadcastReceiver`, `WorkManager`, or notification APIs in the inspected phase sources.

Current Cangjie SDK does provide a callable runtime-permission path through `AtManager.requestPermissionsFromUser(context: UIAbilityContext, permissionList: Array<Permissions>, requestCallback: AsyncCallback<PermissionRequestResult>)` and `PermissionRequestResult`, so this row is closed as phase-specific not-applicable rather than missing-platform-support.

platformContracts.capabilityName: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
platformContracts.minimumParity: Preserve user-visible background behavior, notification/widget state, scheduling semantics, and lifecycle entry points when in source scope.
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-02 source inspection confirmed real app-entry and lifecycle scope, but not Android background services/receivers/widgets: `FoodikeApp.kt` defines `class FoodikeApp : Application()`, `MainActivity.kt` defines a single `ComponentActivity` with `installSplashScreen().setKeepOnScreenCondition { splashViewModel.isLoading.value }`, and `SplashViewModel.kt` resolves onboarding vs home from persisted login state before normal UI rendering.

Current Cangjie SDK declares HarmonyOS app-entry and lifecycle APIs through `UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, and ability startup methods such as `startAbility` / `startAbilityForResult`, which are the direct target replacement for the source `Application` + `Activity` startup surface.

HarmonyOS Cangjie navigation docs recommend ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app page routing, so the source single-activity navigation shell should be implemented as HarmonyOS app bootstrap plus ArkUI navigation rather than Android activity/navigation infrastructure.
platformContracts.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie AbilityKit app-entry APIs (`ohos.app.ability.ui_ability.UIAbility`, `UIAbilityContext`, `Want`) plus ArkUI `Navigation`/`NavPathStack` or `Router` for in-app startup and route flow
platformContracts.targetStrategy: Resolve the source Android platform component surface as a HarmonyOS platform replacement: model app startup in the target `UIAbility`/descriptor entry, keep splash-like gating inside bootstrap state before rendering the main route, and implement onboarding/home navigation using the existing ArkUI route shell instead of Android `Application`, `ComponentActivity`, and `core-splashscreen` APIs.
